item.title: Gilts and European Bonds Surge as Oil Drops on Iran War Optimism
item.description: UK and European government bonds surged, sending yields tumbling, as oil prices slumped on hopes that the Iran war may end in the coming few weeks.

item.title: Nigeria Weighs $5 Billion Swap Deal With UAE Bank as Costs Rise
item.description: Nigeria plans to raise $5 billion from the United Arab Emirates’ largest lender in a derivatives deal to cut borrowing costs, joining a growing number of African nations using the instruments as the war in Iran drives yields higher.

item.title: India Begins Counting Over 1 Billion People in Digital Census
item.description: India has begun its long-delayed national census, a policy-shaping exercise that will collect a wide range of data and, for the first time since independence, include citizens’ caste.

item.title: Oil Slides Below $100 on Optimism Over Iran War Resolution
item.description: Oil tumbled after President Donald Trump again signaled a potential end to the Iran war, even as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed and more US troops head to the region.

item.title: Asian Stocks Rally Most in a Year on Hopes Iran War May End
item.description: Asian stocks rallied the most in nearly a year, tracking Wall Street’s rally on optimism that the war in Iran may end in the near future.

item.title: Australia Spending Holds Up as War Drives Oil Spike, CBA Says
item.description: Australian consumer spending has held up in the face of the Middle East conflict and the resulting surge in oil prices, the nation’s top lender Commonwealth Bank of Australia said.

item.title: Coca-Cola Plans $1 Billion South Africa Investment Through 2030
item.description: Coca-Cola Co. and its two authorized bottling partners in South Africa plan to invest 17.6 billion rand ($1 billion) in domestic operations through 2030 to expand capacity and bolster distribution.

item.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise on Iran War Optimism
European Stock Futures Jump on Optimism For Iran War Conclusion
item.description: European stock futures jumped on Wednesday morning, tracking a rally on Wall Street after President Donald Trump said he foresaw ending the war on Iran within two to three weeks.

item.title: Indian Rupee Set for More Chaos as Banks Unwind $30 Billion in Arbitrage Trades
item.description: The biggest shock to India’s currency market in years is set to worsen as banks prepare to unwind billions of dollars more in arbitrage trades.

item.title: Iran War Hobbles Myanmar Economy as Gasoline Supplies Disappear
item.description: Countries across Asia are limiting consumers’ fuel purchases. Run by a junta and facing civil war, Myanmar is among the hardest hit.

item.title: John Aylward’s Sona Plans Private Credit Push Into Japan Market
item.description: John Aylward’s Sona Asset Management is eyeing Japan for new opportunities and is adding personnel to lead the effort, as the credit manager continues to expand globally.

item.title: Grain Prices Rise as Weak US Plantings Add to Iran War Concerns
item.description: Grain prices rose in Chicago as lower-than-expected US planting figures compounded the food production worries triggered by the Middle East war.

item.title: Asian Credit-Default Swaps Head for Biggest Drop in 11 Months
item.description: The cost of insuring Asian investment-grade debt against default fell the most in 11 months amid signs the Middle-East conflict may be nearing an end.

item.title: Four Passover Questions as the Iran War Nears Endgame
item.description: Both sides are signalling readiness for a deal, but the answers are unclear.

item.title: Indonesia’s B50 Pivot Shows War Is Stoking Global Biofuel Demand
item.description: Indonesia’s abrupt pivot to expand its biodiesel mandate is the latest sign of how the war in Iran is reshaping energy policy, tightening global vegetable oil supplies as more gets funneled into fuel.

item.title: Ferraris, Advil and AI Chips: 10 Companies to Watch Right Now
item.description: Analysts at Bloomberg Intelligence have sifted through thousands of publicly traded companies to highlight a select few worthy of investors’ attention.

item.title: Cheaper Chinese Goods Set to Ease Iran Effect on UK Inflation
item.description: The economic repercussions of Donald Trump’s war on Iran are threatening to hurt Britain more than other major economies, with the Bank of England keeping a close eye on price pressures. Yet one of the US President’s other flagship policies — high tariffs — could have a peculiar side-effect of defending the UK from spiraling inflation.

item.title: Iron Ore Extends Gains After Best Month Since September 2024
item.description: Iron ore advanced after posting its biggest monthly gain since September 2024, supported by tropical storms in Australia and an ongoing pricing dispute between China’s state-run buyer and major miner BHP Group.

item.title: Baidu Robotaxis Freeze in Wuhan, Sparking Flurry of Police Calls
item.description: A number of Baidu Inc.’s Apollo Go robotaxis suddenly stopped on the streets of China’s Wuhan city on Tuesday, leaving passengers stranded and raising concerns about the safety and reliability of autonomous driving technology.

item.title: Japan Squeezes Dwindling Ranks of Smokers to Fund Defense Plans
item.description: Japan’s tobacco taxes rose on Wednesday, as the government turns to the country’s ever-shrinking pool of smokers to help fill a shortfall in funding for its defense buildup.

item.title: BRICS-Led Development Bank Touts Yuan Funding for Global South
item.description: China’s onshore bond market, backed by ample liquidity and a stable currency, is emerging as an attractive funding source for developing economies, according to the BRICS-led New Development Bank.

item.title: Copper Rises as Trump Floats Timeline for US to End Iran Attacks
item.description: Copper rose more than 1% after US President Donald Trump said the war on Iran could end within two to three weeks, sparking a relief rally for riskier assets battered by global growth fears.

item.title: Aditya Birla Trading Arm Beefs Up Iron Ore Desk in Asia
item.description: Aditya Birla Global Trading is expanding its iron ore trading team in Singapore, adding two hires to its growing steelmaking materials desk.

item.title: Emerging Assets Eye First Gain in Five Days on War Easing Hopes
item.description: Emerging-market assets rallied after signals from the US and Iran pointed to a de-escalation in tensions.

item.title: Blackstone, Ares, Rivals Grilled by Congress Over Private Credit
item.description: Wall Street’s biggest private credit houses — including Blackstone Inc. and Ares Management Corp. — are facing pointed questions from the Democratic faction of the House Financial Services Committee, prying into how the firms and their investment funds are marketing, valuing and handling the asset class.

item.title: ‘There’s No Other Choice’: War-Hit Asian Buyers Grab Russian Oil
item.description: Energy-starved Asian nations are taking advantage of US sanction waivers to buy Russian oil to fill gaps caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Australia Passes Law to Trade, Stock Fuel Alongside Rare Earths
item.description: Australia’s parliament passed legislation empowering the country’s export credit agency to physically buy, stockpile and sell fuels alongside critical minerals including rare earths, as the country suffers energy shortages triggered by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Woodside Says North West Shelf Back Online After Cyclone Impact
item.description: Woodside Energy Group Ltd. said liquefied natural gas and domestic gas production has restarted at its North West Shelf facility in Australia after a disruption by Severe Cyclone Narelle late last week.

item.title: Singapore Gets Robotaxis as Grab, WeRide Launch Driverless Cars
item.description: Singapore’s Grab Holdings Ltd. became Southeast Asia’s first ride provider to start a driverless service, betting the technology can help cut costs and prove out robotaxis in dense urban traffic.
